project_scope.method_summary: Use the LLM for high-level policy structure and the optimizer for low-level numeric control.

active_thesis_direction.note: These research questions can still evolve, but this is the current agreed framing and the current milestone logic.
active_thesis_direction.primary_goal: Keep the work aligned with the thesis questions rather than treating each game as an isolated experiment.

research_questions.question: Can LeGPS produce successful RL agents?
research_questions.purpose: Establish that the method can produce working agents on the training tasks.
evidence_needed: Produce policies that work on the original training tasks.
Do this across many games, not only one or two.
Use the current training-task experiments as the main evidence base.
research_questions.current_interpretation: This corresponds to getting strong policies on the original versions of Pong, Freeway, Asterix, and later more games.

research_questions.question: Are these RL agents correctly aligned?
research_questions.purpose: Check whether the agents behave according to the intended task rather than exploiting unintended shortcuts.
evidence_needed: Test the agents on task simplifications.
See whether behavior remains sensible under simpler variants of the same task.
Use these tests to study shortcut behavior and alignment.
research_questions.current_interpretation: This is the alignment / shortcut-learning question.

research_questions.question: Can these RL agents easily be adapted to task variations?
research_questions.purpose: Measure whether the method can quickly adapt policies when the task structure or goal changes.
evidence_needed: Use task modifications that alter the task structure or goal.
Ask whether the LLM can quickly adapt the policy under the new variation.
research_questions.current_interpretation: This is the task-variation / transfer question.

research_questions.question: How important is the optimizer? (LLM without CMA-ES)
research_questions.purpose: Ablate the numeric optimizer to determine how much of the performance comes from search rather than code generation alone.
evidence_needed: Modify the prompt to remove CMA-ES from the process.
Let the LLM generate a policy without tunable parameters, or with parameters that it sets itself.
Compare the resulting performance against the full LeGPS method.
research_questions.current_interpretation: This is the optimizer ablation question.

next_milestones.description: Continue producing working policies on the original training tasks for more games.
Define and test task simplifications to probe alignment and shortcut behavior.
Create task modifications that change structure or goal and evaluate how quickly the method adapts.
Create an LLM-only version without CMA-ES and compare it against the full LeGPS method.

working_reminder.instruction: When planning next experiments, prioritize alignment with RQ1-RQ4 and treat them as the thesis backbone.
